person.name: Elizabeth le Despenser

memoryArtifacts.headline: Coat of Arms (Lozenge) of Beauchamp, Earls of Warwick

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Elizabeth Despenser, the daughter of Hugh Despenser and Isabel Beauchamp, married Ralph Camoys. Their marriage settlement was made 20 May 1316. Elizabeth is believed to have died between 14 March 1327 and 17 February 1331.

«b»Children«/b»
1.) Hugh deCamoys, Knt. he was of age in 1345, died before 1385, married Joan deBrayboef

2.) John deCampys, Knt, born before 1320, he was 40 years old in 1360, died between 5 March 1362 and 1365 when his 2nd wife remarried. John 1st married Margaret Foliot, his 2nd wife was Elizabeth LeLatimer.

3.) Ralph deCampys

4.)Isabel deCampys, (Nun) born before 1323/4, died 1396

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth le Despenser於大約1300年出生在英國哈普爾維古姆時，她的父親Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester40歲，母親Lady Isabella de Beauchamp53歲。她於1316年5月20日之前與Ralph de Camoys結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1331年2月17日之前在英國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth le Despenser于大约1300年出生在英国哈普爾維古姆时，她的父亲Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester40岁，母亲Lady Isabella de Beauchamp53岁。她于1316年5月20日之前与Ralph de Camoys结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1331年2月17日之前在英国。

nameMeanings.name: le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth le Despenser was born about 1300, in Winchester, Hampshire, England, her father, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, was 40 and her mother, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, was 53. She married Ralph de Camoys before 20 May 1316. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 2 daughters. She died before 17 February 1331, in England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth le Despenser est née environ 1300, à Winchester, Hampshire, Angleterre, son père, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, avait 53 ans. Elle a épousé Ralph de Camoys avant 20 mai 1316. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée avant 17 février 1331, à Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth le Despenser um 1300 in Winchester, Hampshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester 40 und ihre Mutter Lady Isabella de Beauchamp 53. Sie hat vor 20. Mai 1316 Ralph de Camoys geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb vor 17. Februar 1331 in England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth le Despenser è nata il circa 1300 nel Winchester, Hampshire, England, sua padre, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, aveva 40 anni e sua madre, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, aveva 53 anni. Lei sposato Ralph de Camoys prima del 20 maggio 1316. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 2 figlie. Morì prima del 17 febbraio 1331, nel England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Despenserが約1300年にイギリスハンプシュアイアウィンチェスターで生まれた時，父Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchesterは40歳，母Lady Isabella de Beauchampは53歳であった。1316年5月20日以前にRalph de Camoysと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1331年2月17日以前にイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth le Despenser(이)가 대략 1300년 영국 함프실르 윈체스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester(은)는 40세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lady Isabella de Beauchamp(은)는 53세였다. 그녀는 1316년 5월 20일 이전 Ralph de Camoys(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1331년 2월 17일 이전 영국에서 사망했다.

mn.about 1300–before 17 February 1331: ойролцоогоор 1300 он–1331 оны Хоёрдугаар сарын 17–наас өмнө

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth le Despenser nasceu aproximadamente 1300, em Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, tinha 40 anos e sua mãe, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, tinha 53 anos. Ela casou-se com Ralph de Camoys antes 20 de maio de 1316. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu antes 17 de fevereiro de 1331, em Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth le Despenser родилась около 1300 г. в местности винчестир, хампшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, 53. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ralph de Camoys перед 20 май 1316 г. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 2 дочери. Она умерла перед 17 февраль 1331 г. в местности Англия.

sm.about 1300–before 17 February 1331: e tusa o le 1300–a'e le'I oo le 17 Fepuari 1331

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth le Despenser nació aproximadamente 1300, en Winchester, Hampshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Hugh le Despenser 1st Earl of Winchester, tenía 40 años y su mamá, Lady Isabella de Beauchamp, tenía 53 años. Se casó con Ralph de Camoys antes de 20 de mayo de 1316. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió antes de 17 de febrero de 1331, en Inglaterra.
